content.full_text:
December 28, 1977

Jerome Fishkin  
Fishkin, Plastiras & Redmond  
5026 Geary Boulevard  
San Francisco, CA 94118

Dear Mr. Fishkin:

In response to your letter of December 12, 1977, I am providing the following advice. You propose a contract whereby the campaign manager of your campaign, Rich Meagher, will be paid $550 owed to him by you and the Fishkin Campaign Committee (I.D. No. 770041). As I understand it, Meagher will be paid through cancellation of a $500 debt owed to T & R Investments and a $60 debt owed to you. You, in turn, will credit T & R in the amount of $550 for attorney's fees.

This proposed transaction does not violate the Political Reform Act. However, as I pointed out in our telephone conversation, the committee should report an expenditure to Meagher and non-monetary contributions by you to the committee in the form of forgivenesses of your loan of $60 (parking tickets) to Meagher and your payment of the debt owed by Meagher to T & R.

I hope this information has been useful. If you have any further questions, please do not hesitate to call me.

Sincerely,

Lee C. Rosenthal  
Acting Chief  
Legal Division

sections.conclusion: This proposed transaction does not violate the Political Reform Act. However, the committee should report an expenditure to Meagher and non-monetary contributions by the candidate to the committee in the form of forgivenesses of the candidate's loan to Meagher and payment of the debt owed by Meagher to T & R.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether a proposed transaction involving campaign manager payment through debt cancellation and attorney fee credit violates the Political Reform Act and what reporting obligations arise.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC concludes the transaction does not violate the Political Reform Act but requires the committee to report an expenditure to the campaign manager and the candidate to report non-monetary contributions representing the forgiveness of loans and debt payments.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION section headers present; content extracted from body text. Letter references prior telephone conversation. Document discusses complex multi-party transaction involving campaign committee, candidate, campaign manager, and third-party investor.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether a proposed transaction involving campaign manager payment through debt cancellation and attorney fee credit violates the Political Reform Act and what reporting obligations arise.

CONCLUSION: This proposed transaction does not violate the Political Reform Act. However, the committee should report an expenditure to Meagher and non-monetary contributions by the candidate to the committee in the form of forgivenesses of the candidate's loan to Meagher and payment of the debt owed by Meagher to T & R.

embedding.summary: FPPC advises that a proposed debt cancellation arrangement to pay a campaign manager does not violate the Political Reform Act, but requires specific reporting of expenditures and non-monetary contributions by both the committee and candidate.
